mission.title: Install and Configure Kagenti
mission.description: Kagenti is a cloud-native middleware designed for deploying and orchestrating AI agents through standardized communication protocols. It simplifies the deployment complexity, enhances security, and reduces operational overhead associated with agent-based applications. Ideal for organizations looking to streamline their AI agent deployment in production environments.

steps.title: Install the Kagenti CLI
steps.description:
This step installs the Kagenti command-line interface, which is necessary for managing the deployment.

```bash

curl -LO https://github.com/kagenti/kagenti/releases/download/v0.5.1/kagenti-cli-linux-amd64 && chmod +x kagenti-cli-linux-amd64 && sudo mv kagenti-cli-linux-amd64 /usr/local/bin/kagenti-cli

```

> This command downloads the CLI binary, makes it executable, and moves it to the system path.
commands: curl -LO https://github.com/kagenti/kagenti/releases/download/v0.5.1/kagenti-cli-linux-amd64 && chmod +x kagenti-cli-linux-amd64 && sudo mv kagenti-cli-linux-amd64 /usr/local/bin/kagenti-cli

steps.title: Create a Kubernetes Cluster
steps.description:
This step creates a new Kubernetes cluster using the Kagenti CLI with the specified version and node count.

```bash

kagenti-cli create cluster --name kagenti-cluster --version 1.30 --node-count 3 --region us-central1

```

> Replace 'us-central1' with your preferred region.

steps.title: Set Up Kubeconfig
steps.description:
This step configures your kubeconfig file to interact with the newly created cluster.

```bash

kagenti-cli kubeconfig --cluster kagenti-cluster

```

> This command sets up the kubeconfig for the created cluster.

steps.title: Verify Cluster Nodes
steps.description:
This step checks the status of the nodes in the cluster to ensure they are running correctly.

```bash

kubectl get nodes

```

> You should see all nodes in the 'Ready' state.

steps.title: Post-Install Configuration
steps.description:
This step sets up autoscaling and monitoring for the Kagenti deployment.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/kagenti/kagenti/v0.5.1/deploy/autoscaling.yaml

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/kagenti/kagenti/v0.5.1/deploy/monitoring.yaml

```

> This command applies the autoscaling configuration.
> This command applies the monitoring configuration.

uninstall.title: Uninstall Kagenti
uninstall.description:
This step removes the Kagenti deployment and associated resources. Be aware that this will result in data loss and orphaned resources.

```bash

kagenti-cli delete cluster --name kagenti-cluster

kubectl delete namespace kagenti

```

> This command deletes the cluster and all associated resources.
> This command deletes the namespace used by Kagenti.

upgrade.title: Upgrade Kagenti
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you back up your configurations and data. This step will upgrade the Kagenti installation to the latest version.

```bash

kubectl drain <node-name> --ignore-daemonsets

kagenti-cli upgrade --version v0.5.1

kubectl uncordon <node-name>

```

> Replace <node-name> with the name of the node you want to drain.
> This command upgrades Kagenti to the specified version.
> This command uncordons the node after the upgrade.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to create cluster'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This may occur due to insufficient permissions or incorrect parameters.

**Fix:**
Ensure you have the necessary permissions and verify the parameters used in the create command.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'kubectl get nodes' returns no nodes
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that the cluster may not have been created successfully.

**Fix:**
Check the cluster creation logs and ensure the cluster is running.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Kubeconfig not found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that the kubeconfig file was not set up correctly.

**Fix:**
Run the kubeconfig setup command again to ensure it is configured properly.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Insufficient resources available'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This may occur if the cluster does not have enough resources allocated.

**Fix:**
Increase the node count or resource limits in your cluster configuration.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for autoscaling and enhanced monitoring capabilities.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: No specific migration steps required for this version.

resolution.summary: You should see output similar to 'NAME STATUS ROLES AGE VERSION' with all nodes listed as 'Ready'.

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running with the specified resources.
